title: Footprints in Blue
synopsis: Oscar explores an ice cavern where someone keeps leaving helpful items in his path. As he returns again and again, he notices new clues that lead him to discover his mysterious helper.

pages.text:
The ice makes tiny clicking sounds when I step on it, like someone tapping their fingernails on a table. I walk into the cavern because the pale blue light from above looks like it's painting everything in watercolor. My breath comes out in small puffs that disappear before I can count to three.

Someone left a warm scarf on a ledge right where I need to climb up. This seems like the kind of thing that doesn't happen by accident, but I put it on anyway. It smells like cinnamon and feels like it was just in someone's pocket.

I look around for whoever dropped it, but the cavern echoes back only my own footsteps. The walls curve up and disappear into shadows that the blue light can't reach. Everything feels bigger when you're alone.

illustration.description: Oscar, a boy with short brown wavy hair, fair skin, and warm hazel eyes, wraps a scarf around himself in an ice cavern. Pale blue light streams from above, illuminating crystalline walls. Bright sunny playground art style with primary colors, white space, joyful energetic atmosphere, inclusive playground elements like colorful climbing holds embedded in the ice.
illustration.alt: Oscar putting on a mysteriously placed scarf in the glowing ice cavern

pages.text:
I climb higher, using the scarf to keep my hands warm on the ice. Near the top, there's a small alcove with something glinting inside. It's my tablet, the one I thought I lost yesterday.

Except it's turned off, and there's a note stuck to the screen that says 'Look up instead' in handwriting I don't recognize. I do look up, and the pale blue light is coming from a crack in the cavern ceiling where real sky shows through.

I put the tablet in my backpack without turning it on. Something about finding it here makes it feel less important than it did yesterday. The crack of sky above seems to pulse with a rhythm, like someone breathing.

illustration.description: Oscar, a boy with short brown wavy hair, fair skin, and warm hazel eyes, reaches toward a tablet in an ice alcove. Above him, pale blue light streams through a crack showing sky. Bright sunny playground art style with primary colors, white space, joyful energetic feeling, inclusive playground elements like monkey bars formed from ice formations.
illustration.alt: Oscar discovering his tablet with a note in an icy alcove

pages.text:
I come back the next day because I keep thinking about that handwriting on the note. It was neat in the way that suggests someone was trying hard to make it readable, like when I write thank-you cards for my grandmother.

The scarf is gone from the ledge, but now there are small stones arranged in a path leading deeper into the cavern than I went yesterday. Each stone is smooth and warm to the touch, which makes no sense in a place made of ice.

I follow the stone path and notice things I missed before. There are tiny holes in the ice walls, perfectly round, like someone drilled them with a very small drill. Some of the holes have icicles growing sideways out of them, which breaks at least two rules of how icicles are supposed to work.

illustration.description: Oscar, a boy with short brown wavy hair, fair skin, and warm hazel eyes, follows a path of smooth stones deeper into the ice cavern. The pale blue light reveals mysterious round holes in the walls with sideways icicles. Bright sunny playground art style with primary colors, white space, joyful energetic atmosphere, inclusive playground elements like stepping stones that look like playground equipment.
illustration.alt: Oscar following mysterious warm stones through the ice cavern

pages.text:
The stone path ends at a wider chamber where someone has built a small fire that somehow doesn't melt the ice around it. Next to the fire sits a girl about my age, poking at the flames with a stick.

'I wondered when you'd get this far,' she says without looking up. 'I'm Maya, and I've been leaving things for you because you looked like you needed to find something.'

I sit down across from the impossible fire. 'I didn't know I was looking for anything.'

'That's usually when the best finding happens,' Maya says, and pokes the fire again. A spark jumps up and stays floating in the air like it forgot about gravity.

illustration.description: Oscar, a boy with short brown wavy hair, fair skin, and warm hazel eyes, sits across from Maya near a magical fire that doesn't melt the surrounding ice. Pale blue light mixes with warm fire glow. Maya has kind eyes and is poking the fire with a stick. Bright sunny playground art style with primary colors, white space, joyful energetic feeling, inclusive diverse kids playing atmosphere with playground swings visible in the ice formations.
illustration.alt: Oscar meeting Maya by an impossible fire in the ice cavern

pages.text:
Maya and I come to the cavern together now, and I notice even more details each time. Today she shows me that the round holes in the walls are actually windows to other parts of the cavern system, like a network of secret passages.

'I found this place when I was trying to get away from my phone,' Maya explains. 'It kept buzzing with notifications, and I wanted somewhere quiet to think.'

We peer through the holes and see chambers filled with things other people have left behind: books with bent covers, art supplies, a chess set with half the pieces missing. Everything looks like it was abandoned in the middle of being used.

'People come here to remember things,' Maya says. 'Things that screens make us forget.'

illustration.description: Oscar, a boy with short brown wavy hair, fair skin, and warm hazel eyes, and Maya peer through round holes in the ice walls, looking into chambers filled with abandoned items. Pale blue light illuminates the scene. Bright sunny playground art style with primary colors, white space, joyful energetic atmosphere, inclusive diverse kids playing, friendship activities visible through the window-holes like playground equipment.
illustration.alt: Oscar and Maya exploring the network of chambers through ice windows

pages.text:
We climb up to the crack in the ceiling where real sky shows through. Maya brings a small mirror and reflects sunlight down into the cavern, making rainbow patterns dance on the ice walls.

'This is better than any video,' I say, watching the colors spin and change.

'And it's different every time,' Maya adds. 'Videos are always the same, but this changes with the clouds and the angle of the sun and whether we're holding the mirror steady or letting it wobble.'

I take my tablet out of my backpack and set it on a ledge, screen-down. The rainbow patterns seem brighter without it glowing in my peripheral vision. Some discoveries, I'm learning, require you to stop looking at other things first.

illustration.description: Oscar, a boy with short brown wavy hair, fair skin, and warm hazel eyes, and Maya create rainbow patterns with a mirror, reflecting sunlight through the ceiling crack. Oscar's tablet sits screen-down on a ledge while they watch dancing colors on ice walls. Pale blue light mixes with rainbow reflections. Bright sunny playground art style with primary colors, white space, joyful energetic feeling, inclusive friendship activities, diverse kids playing atmosphere with slides and swings formed by ice and light.
illustration.alt: Oscar and Maya creating magical rainbow patterns while Oscar's tablet sits forgotten

discussion: How do Oscar and Maya help each other?
What does the ice cavern represent?
Why does Oscar put down his tablet?

Pale blue light streams from above, creating sparkles on the ice walls. In the bright sunny playground art style with primary colors, white space, joyful energetic feeling, showing inclusive park-like elements integrated into the icy scene.
coverPrompt.alt: A boy looking into a magical ice cavern filled with blue light

questions.question: What does Oscar find on the ledge during his first visit?
questions.explanation: Oscar finds a warm scarf that smells like cinnamon on the ledge during his first climb.

questions.question: Who has been leaving helpful things for Oscar?
questions.explanation: Maya reveals that she has been leaving things for Oscar because he looked like he needed to find something.

questions.question: What makes the fire in the cavern unusual?
questions.explanation: The fire is unusual because it somehow doesn't melt the ice around it, which is impossible in real life.

questions.question: What do the round holes in the walls turn out to be?
questions.explanation: Maya shows Oscar that the round holes are actually windows to other parts of the cavern system.

questions.question: How do Oscar and Maya create rainbow patterns?
questions.explanation: Maya uses a small mirror to reflect sunlight down through the ceiling crack, creating dancing rainbow patterns on the ice walls.
